overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from The Witcher 3 showing Geralt walking through a misty forest trail. The character moves steadily along a dirt path surrounded by dense pine trees and grassy undergrowth in atmospheric morning light.

agents.ego_description: Geralt walking steadily along the forest path with two swords strapped to his back

entered.detail: narrow winding dirt trail cutting through the forest floor
tall evergreen pine trees forming a dense forest wall
tall evergreen pine trees forming a dense forest wall
calm lake water visible through gaps in the trees on the right side
tall green grass and shrubs lining both sides of the path
tall green grass and shrubs lining both sides of the path

segments.scene_description: Geralt walks along a narrow dirt trail through a misty pine forest. The path runs alongside a calm lake visible on the right, with dense evergreen trees and grassy undergrowth surrounding the trail in the diffused morning light.

events.description: a small fox runs across the dirt path from left to right

agents.ego_description: Geralt continues walking steadily along the winding forest path

notable_agents.action: fox running quickly across the path from left to right into the undergrowth

segments.scene_description: The path moves deeper into the forest interior away from the lake. At around 24 seconds, a fox suddenly runs across the trail from left to right, disappearing into the dense undergrowth as Geralt continues his steady walk.

scene_state.functional_zone: rocky forest path with fallen timber

agents.ego_description: Geralt walking through a denser section of forest with rocky terrain and fallen logs

entered.detail: large moss-covered boulders and rocks beside the trail
decaying tree trunk lying across the grassy ground

segments.scene_description: The trail continues through increasingly rugged terrain with moss-covered rocks and fallen logs appearing beside the path. The forest remains dense with tall pines and thick undergrowth as Geralt maintains his steady pace.

scene_state.functional_zone: forest path passing ancient stone ruins

events.description: ancient stone ruins become visible on the left side of the forest path

agents.ego_description: Geralt walking past ancient stone ruins while continuing along the forest trail

entered.detail: ancient weathered stone wall ruins partially covered in moss and vegetation

segments.scene_description: Ancient stone ruins emerge on the left side of the path, suggesting former habitation in the forest. Geralt walks past the weathered stone walls as they recede into the background, continuing along the winding trail through the misty pine forest.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement through a misty pine forest that shifts from an open lakeside trail into a denser woodland interior. It begins with Geralt walking along a narrow dirt path, where the diffused morning light illuminates a calm lake bordering the right side of the trail. As the camera follows his steady pace, the path gradually veers away from the water and pushes deeper into the thick evergreen trees and grassy undergrowth. The scene then becomes more enclosed by the forest canopy just as a fox suddenly darts across the trail from left to right, disappearing into the brush before the camera continues its forward progression into the woods.

The shot follows a continuous camera movement along a misty forest trail that evolves from a rugged natural path into a landscape marked by ancient ruins. It begins as the trail grows increasingly rugged, winding past moss-covered rocks and fallen logs nestled among dense pines and thick undergrowth. As Geralt maintains his steady pace, the natural wilderness gradually gives way to signs of past habitation. The scene then shifts to reveal weathered stone ruins emerging on the left side of the path, which the camera tracks as the walls recede into the background. Finally, the movement continues along the winding trail, disappearing further into the atmospheric haze of the pine forest.
